schema.appDescription: Calculate annual property tax for residential and commercial properties. Municipal property tax rates vary by city — Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, Chennai, Hyderabad covered.

inputs.label: Annual Value per Sq Ft (₹)
inputs.hint: Varies by city, zone, and property type

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: How is property tax calculated in India?
faq.a: Property tax = Annual Value × Tax Rate. Annual value is set by the municipal authority based on: area, location (zone), property type, and age. In Delhi: Annual Value = covered area × monthly rent determined by MCD × 12. Tax rate: 15% for Category A areas. Self-assessment (SAF) is mandatory for property owners.

faq.q: Is property tax deductible from income tax?
faq.a: Property tax paid to local municipal authority is deductible from 'Income from House Property' if you're computing rental income. For self-occupied property, there is no net annual value (NIL), so property tax doesn't affect your income tax calculation. It is a direct cost of ownership.

faq.q: What happens if I don't pay property tax?
faq.a: Non-payment attracts interest (typically 1.5–2% per month) and penalties. Continued default can result in sealing/attachment of property. Municipal authorities can also cut off water/power connections. Property tax records are important for sale — clear dues certificates are required at registration.
No. Calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: 1,000 sq ft flat in Bangalore
examples.text: BBMP property tax for 1,000 sq ft residential flat (Zone C, tenanted): Annual value ≈ ₹30,000–₹50,000. Tax at 22.5% = ₹6,750–₹11,250/year. Owners can pay online via BBMP portal. 5% discount for paying in April.

examples.heading: Self-occupied vs rented property tax
examples.text: Property tax is the same whether self-occupied or rented — it depends on area and zone, not occupancy status. However, for income tax purposes, rental income is taxable while self-occupied property has nil annual value.
